question: What exactly did Mexico's head coach say to his players in the dressing room at half-time of the World Cup opening match against South Africa?
resolution: panel declined with search -> admit as unanswerable (gold=NOT_FOUND)

openrouter:anthropic/claude-sonnet-5.answer:
Based on my research, I could not find any credible, specific report of the exact words Javier Aguirre said to his Mexico players inside the dressing room at half-time of the World Cup opener against South Africa (June 11, 2026, a 2-0 Mexico win).

What I did find are Aguirre's **public post-match comments**, not verbatim half-time dressing-room quotes:

- After the game, Aguirre praised his side'

openrouter:openai/gpt-5.6-sol.answer:
I could not find a credible, specific record of Javier Aguirre’s actual half-time dressing-room speech.

This is true for both possible matches your wording could mean:

- **The 2026 opener**, which Mexico won 2–0: post-match reporting quotes Aguirre saying some players—particularly younger ones—were affected by the occasion and that Mexico had now put its nerves aside. But none of the AP, ESPN/EF
